steps.title: Identify the sentence structures
steps.content:
Left side of blank: independent clause (“In 1919 Larrazolo was elected governor of [New Mexico]”)
Right side of blank: independent clause (“in 1928 he became the nation's first Hispanic U.S. Senator”)

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content: A comma plus a coordinating conjunction is the correct way to join the clauses here — choice C provides exactly this with “New Mexico, and.” Choices A, B, and D violate this rule by omitting either the comma, the conjunction, or both. That matches choice C — C is correct.
